title: If formula & subtracting dates
excerpt:
i am getting ‘true’ where i think i should be getting a #value. 
i am trying to calculate age for multiple status. 
if status is ‘delivered’ then calculate days between ‘sold’ date and ‘grounding’ date. 
if not status is…

posts.author: StephenP (Stephen Prince)
posts.cookedHtml:
i am getting ‘true’ where i think i should be getting a #value.

i am trying to calculate age for multiple status.

if status is ‘delivered’ then calculate days between ‘sold’ date and ‘grounding’ date.

if not status is not ‘delivered’ but is ‘grounded’ then calculate days between today and ‘grounding’ date.

if any other status other than ‘delivered’ or ‘grounded’ the age should be blank

my current formula: IF({Status}=“Delivered”,DAYS({SOLD DATE},{GROUNDED DATE},IF({Status}=“Grounded”,DAYS({GROUNDED DATE},TODAY(),“”))))

monday pic402×767 28.2 KB

posts.author: StephenP (Stephen Prince)
posts.cookedHtml: i appreciate any help getting this formula correct!

posts.author: StephenP (Stephen Prince)
posts.cookedHtml: @JCorrell i have seen your great help for others…have any ideas on my formula? it seems as if the different groups is the issue. i can do a ‘one’ if statement and that particular status/group works perfectly (showing blank value for the ‘“”’)…but all other status/groups only show ‘true’

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@StephenP

Looks to be just a couple of misplaced ")"s.

Try this:

IF({Status}="Delivered",
  DAYS({SOLD DATE},{GROUNDED DATE}),
  IF({Status}="Grounded",
    DAYS({GROUNDED DATE},TODAY())
    ,""
  )  
)

Jim - The Monday Man

posts.cookedHtml: thank you very much. happy new year
